narrative.headline: 2022 Chevy Traverse: power train trouble dominates owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 90 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is the standout concern at 42% of reports, tied to 1 crash and 4 injuries. Engine and electrical issues follow, each at 18%, then service brakes at 8% and forward collision avoidance at 4%. No fires and no deaths were reported.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The top complaint area by far: 38 reports (42%), tied to 1 crash and 4 injuries. Test how the car shifts and drives under load before buying.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 16 reports (18%), with 1 injury. Watch for any stalling or loss of power on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Tied for second: 16 reports (18%). No crashes or injuries reported, but check that the car starts reliably and that dashboard warnings are clear.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Smaller but serious: 7 reports (8%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Worth a careful brake check despite the lower count.

issues.sample: As I was pulling out of my parking space on the street a car was coming toward me and the forward collision alert lights and sound went off as well as a terrible grinding started as if it was trying to brake as I was driving but not slowing me down. This has now happened 4 times.
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Least reported here: 4 reports (4%), with no crashes or injuries. Confirm the collision alert behaves normally during a test drive.
